title: AI Curator
text: AI Curator The AI Word Curator turns raw player submissions into a clean, canonical bingo word pool. It pairs one Gemini call for fuzzy classification with a deterministic aggregation step that counts votes, assigns founders, and seeds filler words so every pool reaches its target size. Overview Source lives in agent/src/ai/curator.ts . The module exports two functions and the types that describe the pool: Export Kind Role curate(args) async Full pipeline: calls Gemini, then aggregates into a final pool. aggregate(subsSorted, decisions, suggested, poolSize) pure Deterministic vote counting, founder tracking, and filler seeding. Submission type { raw, wallet, ts } — one raw player entry. CuratedWord type { word, votes, founder, aiSuggested } — one pool slot. Decision type Per submission audit record (see below). CurationResult type { pool, decisions } returned by curate . A submission is a single word or short phrase a player predicts a speaker will say out loud during a live event. Curation collapses synonyms, drops spam, and rounds out the pool with AI suggested words. The Gemini Call curate() builds a prompt from the event theme , description , and the timestamp sorted submissions, then delegates to structured() in agent/src/ai/gemini.ts . That helper: Loads the model from config.model and sets the curator's SYSTEM text as the systemInstruction . Forces JSON output via genera

title: HTTP API
text: HTTP API The Bingocle agent ships an Express service ( agent/src/server.ts ) that exposes the AI pipeline and on chain writes as a small REST API. It is the bridge between off chain surfaces (the web app, the Telegram bot, email) and the Mantle contracts. See /agent/overview for how the curator, odds engine, and oracle fit together. Server Basics Default port: 8787 , overridable with the PORT env var ( config.port ). CORS: enabled for all methods used here ( GET,POST,OPTIONS ). The allowed origin comes from CORS ORIGIN and defaults to for the open testnet demo. Body parsing: JSON only, with a 1mb limit. Errors: every handler is wrapped so any thrown error is logged and returned as a blanket HTTP 500 with shape { "error": " " } . A few endpoints return 400 with a reason for bad input. Endpoints Method Path Body Purpose GET /health none Liveness probe; returns chain + model info. POST /events CreateEventInput (+ theme , rewardFundMnt ) Create an event on chain and auto fund its reward pool. GET /events none List all locally stored event records. GET /events/:id none Return the stored record plus live on chain phase . POST /events/:id/submit { word, player? } Append a predicted word to the event's submission inbox. POST /events/:id/commit pool { theme?, description?, submissions? } Run curate → odds → commit the frozen pool on chain. POST /events/:id/validate { transcript, minConf

title: Odds Engine
text: Odds Engine The AI Odds Engine assigns every curated word an opening price and a payout multiplier . It asks Gemini to estimate how likely each word is to be spoken, then a deterministic, pure math blend() mixes that estimate with community vote share into on chain odds. Overview The engine lives in agent/src/ai/odds.ts and exposes two functions: Function Role computeOdds(args) Calls Gemini for per word probabilities, then runs blend() . Returns WordOdds[] . blend(pool, probByWord, vig) Pure odds math: turns probabilities and votes into prices and multipliers. No I/O. Each result is a WordOdds record: Prices and multipliers are emitted in 1e4 fixed point : the SCALE constant from agent/src/config.ts is 10 000 , so 10000 means 1.0 (a price of 1.0 or a 1.0x multiplier). LLM Probability Estimate computeOdds builds a prompt from the event theme , description , and the curated word list, then calls structured() from agent/src/ai/gemini.ts . The system prompt instructs the model to return, for each word, the probability in the range 0..1 that a speaker on the theme says it out loud at least once. Theme central words score high ( 0.8 0.95 ); niche or surprising words score low ( 0.1 0.3 ). The response is validated against a Zod schema ( estimates: [{ word, aiProb }] ) and each aiProb is clamped into [0, 1] . Words missing from the model's reply fall back to a default probability of 0

title: Agent Service Overview
text: Agent Service Overview The Bingocle agent service is a Node/TypeScript process that hosts the three AI roles and acts as the organizer/oracle on chain signer. It bridges off chain language models to Mantle: it curates word pools, prices odds, validates live transcripts, and commits the results with the agent wallet. What It Does The service plays two parts at once: AI layer — it runs three roles backed by Google Gemini: the Word Curator , the Odds Engine , and the Validation Oracle . Signer — its single wallet is the registered ERC 8004 oracle operator and the WordPool curator, so it is authorized to send the commitPool and commitValidation style transactions. The agent wallet is privileged. It must be the registered oracle operator in AgentIdentity and the WordPool curator, and it must hold testnet MNT for gas. See Trust Model and Security for why this is the trust anchor of the system. Source Modules The core wiring lives in agent/src . Each module owns one concern: Module Role config.ts Loads env, exposes typed config , defines SCALE chain.ts ethers v6 provider, signer, contract factories, hash helpers store.ts Local JSON event store ( agent/.data/events.json ) server.ts Express HTTP API (the surface the Capability calls) ai/gemini.ts Gemini client used by all three roles ai/curator.ts Word Curator role ai/odds.ts Odds Engine role ai/oracle.ts Validation Oracle role Configur

title: Validation Oracle
text: Validation Oracle The AI Validation Oracle is the referee: it matches the committed word pool against a live transcript and writes confident verdicts on chain to the Oracle Registry. It pairs an LLM matcher with optional speech to text, but listening is staged from recorded text or an uploaded file rather than a live stream. Overview The oracle runs as a short pipeline. A transcript chunk plus the numbered pool words go to the LLM, which reports which words were genuinely spoken. The agent filters and de duplicates those matches, keeps only the confident ones, and commits each as an on chain validation. Match — matchTranscript() in agent/src/ai/oracle.ts calls Gemini against a transcript snippet. Orchestrate — validateTranscript() in agent/src/services/oracleRun.ts skips already validated words, applies the confidence threshold, and commits. Transcribe — transcribeAudio() in agent/src/ai/stt.ts converts an audio file to text when the audio CLI is used. The on chain commits are real and trigger card marking plus settlement. There is no live audio streaming pipeline — no microphone, RTMP, or WebRTC capture. The demo feeds pre written text or a single uploaded file, so live listening is staged. See the Trust Model for what this means for fairness. Matching the Transcript matchTranscript() accepts the transcript chunk, the canonical pool (array index equals the on chain wordIndex )

title: Chat Commands
headings: The Submit then Finalize Inbox Flow
text: Chat Commands The Bingocle Capability exposes a small, channel agnostic command core. Every verb lives once in commands.ts and is reused by the Telegram bot, the email adapter, and the natural language layer, so all surfaces behave identically. Command Core capability/src/commands.ts defines a single dispatch(userKey, cmd, rest) function plus one cmd handler per verb. The userKey identifies a player (a Telegram user id or an email address) and maps to that user's embedded testnet demo wallet via walletFor(userKey) — see Wallets and NLU. The canonical verb list is the exported VERBS array — 14 verbs in total: isVerb(cmd) strips a leading / and lower cases before matching, so /buy , buy , and BUY all resolve to the same handler. The Verbs Each verb is either a pure agent API call, a direct on chain transaction signed by the per user wallet, or local text. The table below lists what each does and where the work happens. Verb What it does On chain / Agent API start Print the intro + command list ( START TEXT ) Local text help Same as start Local text agentguide Print the full operator guide ( AGENT GUIDE ) Local text wallet Show the user's demo wallet address + MNT balance RPC read ( provider().getBalance ) create Organizer: create an event, returns eventId + txHash , seeds an empty inbox Agent API POST /events submit Store up to 3 predicted words in the in memory inbox keyed by ev

title: Capability Overview
headings: Split Architecture: Organizer vs Player Verbs
text: Capability Overview The Capability is Bingocle's consumer surface — the Part B deliverable — a Minds Bazaar skill that lets anyone play the entire game over chat. It is a Telegraf based Telegram bot with a second email channel that is coded but not yet wired, so Telegram is the live surface. What It Is The Capability wraps the whole game loop into a conversation: create or join an event, submit and found words, buy positions on a bonding curve, mint a bingo card NFT, watch it mark live as the AI oracle validates, and claim winnings. Every chat user gets a per user embedded testnet demo wallet (no seed phrase, no browser extension), so onboarding feels like Web2. Players talk in plain language (for example, "show my wallet", "buy 2 shares of airdrop in event 1") or use the precise slash commands — both work. See Commands for the full verb list and Wallets and NLU for how demo wallets are derived and how free text is mapped to a verb. The Manifest capability.json is the Minds Bazaar manifest that describes the skill to the marketplace. Field Value name Bingocle publicId bingocle version 0.1.0 channels ["telegram", "email"] chain Mantle , Sepolia testnet , chainId 5003 naturalLanguage true track Consumer & Viral DApps (Animoca Minds) The manifest also declares the published commands array (with each command's name , who , optional args , and summary ) plus the Telegram handle @Bin

title: Wallets & NLU
headings: Email Surface (Coded, Not Wired By Default)
text: Wallets & NLU The Bingocle Capability gives every chat user a wallet without a seed phrase and lets them play in plain English. This page covers the two supporting subsystems: per user demo wallets ( capability/src/wallets.ts ) and natural language understanding ( capability/src/nlu.ts ), plus two helpers — on chain word lookup ( chain.ts ) and the optional email surface ( email.ts ). Per User Demo Wallets Every player gets a deterministic testnet wallet derived on the fly from one master mnemonic plus their user key — the Telegram user id or the email sender address. There is no signup, no extension, and no seed phrase shown to the user: a Web2 style onboarding flow on top of a Web3 backend. walletFor(userId) in wallets.ts does the derivation: Step by step: The user key is hashed with ethers.id(key) (keccak256 of the UTF 8 string) to produce a uint256 . That hash is reduced modulo 2 000 000 000 (roughly 2e9 ) to pick a non hardened HD child index. The master HDNodeWallet.fromPhrase(...) derives that child with deriveChild(idx) , then .connect(provider()) binds it to the configured network. The result is cached in an in process Map , so repeated calls for the same user are free and stable. The provider is built from MANTLE RPC URL (default https://rpc.sepolia.mantle.xyz ) and CHAIN ID (default 5003 ) — Mantle Sepolia . The master phrase comes from the DEMO WALLET MNEMONIC envir

title: AgentIdentity
text: AgentIdentity AgentIdentity is an ERC 8004 style identity NFT for AI agents on Mantle. It is soulbound to an operator wallet and carries portable, on chain reputation, so an agent's track record accrues to the token and travels with the wallet — the Turing Test thesis of off chain inference with on chain accountability. Overview The contract is an ERC721 (named Bingocle Agent Identity , symbol BAGENT ) plus Ownable . It binds an operator wallet to a reputation bearing NFT and serves two jobs: Oracle gating — isOracle(who) is the membership check that OracleRegistry consults before accepting a verdict. Reputation accrual — a single authorized writer (the OracleRegistry) records every validation, every event served, and every upheld dispute against the agent's agentId . Each operator can hold at most one identity. agentIdOf[operator] returns 0 when an address has no identity, and a non zero agentId otherwise. Registration And Gating registerAgent mints an identity to an operator wallet. Only the contract owner can call it, and an operator may only be registered once. Function Caller Description registerAgent(address operator, string uri) owner Mints a new identity NFT to operator , assigns agentId = ++agentCount , sets operatorOf[agentId] and agentIdOf[operator] , stores uri , and emits AgentRegistered . Returns the new agentId . Reverts ZeroOperator if operator is address(0) , o

title: BingoCardNFT
headings: Card Layout and the FREE Center
text: BingoCardNFT BingoCardNFT is a soulbound ERC 721 that issues one bingo card per (eventId, player) . Each card's 5x5 layout is shuffled deterministically from an on chain seed and frozen at mint, so the token is the canonical board that settlement reads. Overview The contract ( contracts/src/BingoCardNFT.sol ) extends OpenZeppelin's ERC721 and implements IBingoCardNFT . It is named "Bingocle Card" with symbol "BCARD" and holds a single immutable reference to the IEventFactory , which it uses to resolve the WordPool and OracleRegistry module addresses at runtime. Internal state: cells — mapping(uint256 = uint8[25]) , the frozen card layout per tokenId . eventOf — public mapping(uint256 = uint256) , tokenId to eventId . cardOf — mapping(uint256 = mapping(address = uint256)) , the one card per (eventId, player) . tokenCount — public running counter; the next minted tokenId is ++tokenCount , so token IDs start at 1 and 0 means "no card". Minting mint(uint256 eventId) mints the caller's card and returns the new tokenId . It enforces, in order: 1. factory.exists(eventId) — else reverts NotExist . 2. WordPool.isCommitted(eventId) — else reverts NotCommitted . 3. factory.phaseOf(eventId) is Phase.Founder or Phase.Market — else reverts BadPhase . 4. cardOf[eventId][msg.sender] == 0 — else reverts AlreadyMinted (one card per wallet per event). The seed is computed on chain as: After build

title: Deployment
headings: Render Blueprint For The Node Services
text: Deployment This page documents the full Bingocle deploy pipeline: the Foundry script that deploys and wires the seven contracts, the shell wrappers that auto distribute addresses, contract verification on Mantlescan, and the Render Blueprint for the two Node services. For a faster walkthrough see Quick Start; for running everything against a local chain see Local Development. The Deploy Script contracts/script/Deploy.s.sol is a Foundry Script that deploys all seven contracts in one broadcast, wires them together, and registers the AI agent's ERC 8004 identity. It reads two environment variables: Env var Required Behavior PRIVATE KEY yes Deployer key; the resulting address becomes owner of the ownable modules AGENT ADDRESS no AI agent wallet (oracle operator + word pool curator); via vm.envOr it defaults to the deployer The script runs these steps inside a single vm.startBroadcast / vm.stopBroadcast block: 1. Deploy AgentIdentity(deployer) , EventFactory(deployer) , WordPool(deployer, factory) , WordMarket(factory) , OracleRegistry(deployer, factory) , BingoCardNFT(factory) , and RewardVault(factory) . 2. Call factory.wireModules(wordPool, wordMarket, oracle, cardNFT, rewardVault, agentIdentity) once to populate the factory's module registry. 3. Call agentIdentity.setUpdater(address(oracle)) so the OracleRegistry becomes the only writer of agent reputation. 4. Call agentIdentity

title: EventFactory
text: EventFactory EventFactory is the source of truth for event lifecycle, configuration, and scheduling, and it doubles as the registry of every module address. Each event is keyed by an incrementing id (there is no per event deploy), and its current phase is derived purely from on chain timestamps so all deadlines are contract enforced with no external oracle calls. Module Registry After the modules are deployed, the owner wires them once via wireModules . Every other contract reads its sibling addresses back from the factory rather than holding them independently, so the factory is the single registry of truth. The call is onlyOwner and guarded by the modulesWired flag: a second call reverts AlreadyWired , and any zero address reverts ZeroModule . On success it sets each address, flips modulesWired to true , and emits ModulesWired . Public getter Module wordPool() AI curated word pool (/contracts/word pool) wordMarket() Parimutuel trading market (/contracts/word market) oracleRegistry() Validation verdict log cardNFT() Bingo card ERC 721 rewardVault() Stake escrow and payouts agentIdentity() Agent identity NFT Note: the factory exposes the module addresses but never calls them. Wiring is a one time bootstrap step; the field stored at deploy is the owner, set through Ownable(owner ) . Creating an Event Any caller may create an event and becomes its organizer . The reward pool is f

title: OracleRegistry
text: OracleRegistry OracleRegistry is the permanent on chain audit log of AI Validation Oracle verdicts and the single source of on chain truth for which words were spoken. Settlement and card marking read only the registry's validatedBitmap — never the AI directly — so every payout traces back to a signed, timestamped, disputable on chain record. Overview An ERC 8004 agent (see AgentIdentity) writes its verdicts here, and that write is what triggers downstream settlement. Each verdict is stored in a Verdict struct keyed by (eventId, wordIndex) : The two canonical, machine readable views are a packed bitmap and a counter: validatedBitmap[eventId] — a uint256 where bit w is set if word index w is validated as spoken (post dispute). validatedCount[eventId] — the number of currently validated words for an event. Consumers (WordMarket, BingoCardNFT, RewardVault) read validatedBitmap / validatedCount exclusively. The AI's opinion only counts once it is committed on chain and survives the dispute window. The contract is Ownable and holds an immutable reference to the IEventFactory , from which it resolves the WordPool and AgentIdentity module addresses at call time. Committing a Verdict commitValidation(eventId, wordIndex, confidence, snippet) records that a word was spoken. It enforces a strict set of preconditions before writing: Check Revert Meaning id.isOracle(msg.sender) NotOracle Ca

title: Smart Contracts Overview
text: Smart Contracts Overview Bingocle runs on seven Solidity contracts deployed to Mantle . Together they own the full game lifecycle: event scheduling, an AI curated word pool, a fee free trading market, an on chain oracle log, soulbound bingo cards, a reward vault, and reputation bearing agent identities. Build Configuration All contracts share one toolchain, pinned in contracts/foundry.toml . They are written against OpenZeppelin ( Ownable , ERC721 , ERC20 / SafeERC20 , ReentrancyGuard , Math , Base64 , Strings ). Setting Value solc 0.8.28 evm version cancun via ir true optimizer true , optimizer runs = 200 Networks mantle (chain 5000 ), mantle sepolia (chain 5003 ) See Deployment for RPC, verifier, and wiring details. The Seven Contracts Contract Role Page EventFactory Event lifecycle, config, and timestamp derived phase scheduling; one time wireModules registers every module address. Event Factory WordPool Curator committed final word list as keccak hashes, with opening prices and payout multipliers in 1e4 scale plus per word founder designations; frozen behind a merkle root. Word Pool WordMarket Per word parimutuel trading on a linear bonding curve, fee free, plus settlement and redemption. Word Market OracleRegistry Permanent, auditable log of AI oracle verdicts with confidence, snippet, and a dispute window. Oracle Registry BingoCardNFT Soulbound 5x5 cards with a determinis

title: RewardVault
text: RewardVault RewardVault is the pool funded reward rail of Bingocle, kept entirely separate from the buyer payouts that settle on WordMarket. It custodies a per event sponsor pool and, at settlement, pays two things from that pool: tiered bingo bonuses earned on a player's card and founder free seed payouts for words a founder seeded that later validated. Overview The contract is a ReentrancyGuard that reads all lifecycle and config state from the EventFactory ( factory ). It holds one pool per event in poolBalance[eventId] and never touches WordMarket reserves, so sponsor money can never come from buyers' stakes. Two pieces of accounting prevent double payment: claimedTiers[eventId][user] — a uint8 bitmask of bonus tiers already paid to a user. seedPaid[eventId][word][founder] — a bool marking a founder's seed for a word as paid. Bonuses and seeds are paid from the pre funded pool only . If the pool cannot cover a claim, the whole claim reverts PoolUnderfunded — partial payment is never made. Functions Function Access Phase Effect fund(eventId, amount) Organizer or any sponsor Submission , Founder , Market Deposits amount into poolBalance[eventId] ; reverts AfterSettlement once the event has gone Live or later claim(eventId) Any player or founder Settled Pays newly earned bingo bonus plus unpaid founder seed; reverts NotSettled , NothingToClaim , or PoolUnderfunded withdrawResi

title: WordMarket
text: WordMarket WordMarket is Bingocle's dynamic parimutuel trading engine. Each word in an event is an independent tradable outcome: buying mints shares along a linear bonding curve, selling burns them, and at settlement every validated word's holders split that word's reserve plus a pro rata cut of the losing words' reserves. The design keeps the contract solvent by construction, which is enforced by a stateful invariant fuzzer. Bonding Curve Each word has its own linear price curve price = base + slope supply / ONE , so price rises with demand. The first time a word is touched, ensureCurve freezes the curve parameters and never changes them again: base is set from the WordPool opening price: base = priceOf(eventId, w) PRICE UP (lifting the 1e4 scale price to 1e18 ). slope = mulDiv(base, ONE, TARGET SHARES) (clamped to a minimum of 1 ), so the price roughly doubles after TARGET SHARES (about 1000e18 ) shares are minted. The opening price comes from WordPool.priceOf — see WordPool. Words priced and curated by the AI Curator therefore open with different base values. The cost to move supply from s0 to s1 is the path independent integral of the curve, implemented in cost : Because cost is the exact area under the curve, reserve[eventId][w] always equals that area, and the contract's token balance always equals totalReserve[eventId] == sum of reserve[w] . Trading Trading is fee free :

title: WordPool
text: WordPool WordPool stores the AI curated final word list and odds for an event. The Curator agent commits it once, before the market opens, and it is frozen thereafter so the playable word set and opening prices cannot change mid event. Overview Each event has exactly one pool, keyed by eventId . A pool holds four parallel arrays indexed by word index : the keccak256 hash of each canonical word, its opening price, its payout multiplier, and the founder address that first seeded it. The plaintext words live off chain in the agent store; only their hashes are stored on chain, bound by a merkle root. The contract is Ownable . The constructor takes (address owner , address factory ) and records the immutable EventFactory reference used to validate event existence and read deadlines. The pool is produced by the AI Curator, and its prices and multipliers are set by the Odds Engine. The committed values feed directly into the WordMarket trading surface. Roles Role How it is set Capability owner constructor ( Ownable ) call setCurator curator setCurator(address c) , onlyOwner call commitPool The curator is not set in the constructor — the owner assigns it via setCurator , which emits CuratorSet(curator) . In practice the owner points it at the agent wallet after deploy. Committing A Pool Parameter Type Meaning eventId uint256 Target event (must exist in the factory). wordHashes bytes32[

title: Frontend Overview
text: Frontend Overview The Bingocle frontend is a Next.js (App Router) web app with a marketing landing page and a dual mode bingo game: a fully client side demo and a live on chain game backed by contracts on Mantle Sepolia. This page covers the stack, providers, and route to component map; for the wiring details see Routes and Web3. Stack The app lives under frontend/ and is built on a deliberately recent toolchain — note this is not the Next.js most training data describes. Concern Choice Notes Framework next@16.2.9 App Router, React Server Components, SSR enabled UI runtime react@19.2.4 / react dom@19.2.4 Chain reads/writes wagmi@^2.19.5 + viem@^2.52.2 Hooks based contract access Wallet UI @rainbow me/rainbowkit@^2.2.11 Dark theme, custom palette Data/cache @tanstack/react query@^5.101.0 Backs wagmi's async state Styling tailwindcss@^4 (via @tailwindcss/postcss ) Fonts Cinzel , Cinzel Decorative , Cormorant Garamond Loaded via next/font/google in app/layout.tsx Charts Custom SVG ( components/app/PriceChart.tsx ) Hand built path/area, no charting library The repo's AGENTS.md warns that this Next.js version has breaking changes versus older releases — consult node modules/next/dist/docs/ before editing app code. Scripts in frontend/package.json : next dev , next build , next start , and eslint (run as npm run dev / build / start / lint ). Providers app/layout.tsx is the root layou

title: Routes & Web3
text: Routes & Web3 The Bingocle frontend is a Next.js app with three routes — a marketing landing page, a self contained demo simulation, and the live on chain game — wired to Mantle through wagmi and RainbowKit. This page documents each route and exactly how the live game reads and writes contracts. Routes Path Component Description / app/page.tsx Landing page composed of TopBar , Hero , SectionNav , AboutSection , HowToPlay , GamePreview , BetaSignup , and Footer . /play app/play/page.tsx → DemoGame Pure client side simulation. No wallet or chain required. /app app/app/page.tsx → OnchainGame The live game wired to the Mantle Sepolia contracts. Both /play and /app render a single "use client" component inside a dark full screen main . See Frontend Overview for how these fit together. The landing page outline lists AboutSection between SectionNav and HowToPlay ; the actual JSX renders AboutSection , HowToPlay , and GamePreview inside the midlands main, with BetaSignup and Footer following. Wallet & Chain Config lib/wagmi.ts builds the connector config with RainbowKit's getDefaultConfig : Chains : mantleSepoliaTestnet is listed first (the default target), followed by mantle . Both come from wagmi/chains . initialChain : exported as mantleSepoliaTestnet . ssr: true : enables server side rendering support. WalletConnect projectId : read from NEXT PUBLIC WALLETCONNECT PROJECT ID , falli

title: Architecture
text: Architecture Bingocle is a four layer system: smart contracts on Mantle Sepolia, a Node/TypeScript agent service that runs the AI and signs organizer transactions, a Telegram capability with per user demo wallets, and a Next.js frontend. This page explains each layer and the integration points that wire them together. The Four Layers The four layers share no runtime ABI validation . Each layer ships its own ABI strategy (see below), so an address or ABI mismatch surfaces only at call time. Subsystems Subsystem Responsibility Stack Contracts Markets, cards, oracle verdicts, rewards, agent identity Solidity 0.8.28 (Foundry), Mantle Sepolia Agent AI Curator, Odds Engine, Validation Oracle; signs organizer/AI txs Node/TS, Express, ethers , Gemini Capability Telegram chat surface, NLU, per user wallets Node/TS, ethers Frontend Public web app: live market + cards Next.js, wagmi/viem See /contracts/overview, /agent/overview, /capability/overview, and /frontend/overview for each layer in depth. The Contract Registry The deploy script contracts/script/Deploy.s.sol deploys all 7 contracts, then calls factory.wireModules(...) once to register the addresses of WordPool , WordMarket , OracleRegistry , BingoCardNFT , RewardVault , and AgentIdentity on the EventFactory . Every module is constructed with address(factory) and resolves its siblings through that registry, so the factory is the si

title: Introduction
text: Introduction Bingocle = Bing o + Or acle — a community bingo prediction market where the entire game loop is run and refereed by AI agents on the Mantle Network . The audience of any live event — a conference, podcast, debate, livestream, or demo day — proposes words they predict a speaker will say. AI curates those words into a pool and into bingo cards, each word becomes an on chain prediction asset traded on a bonding curve, and an AI Validation Oracle listens to the event live (speech to text + LLM) and writes its verdicts straight to a smart contract . That single on chain write triggers settlement, card marking, and reward distribution — automatically and transparently. No admin marks words by hand. No black box. Every AI decision is recorded permanently on chain — embodying the hackathon's Turing Test theme: on chain benchmarking of AI, agent identity, and radical transparency. Hackathon: The Turing Test Hackathon 2026 (Phase II — AI Awakening) · Mantle Network · Track 04 — Consumer & Viral DApps (Animoca Minds). Two coupled deliverables Bingocle ships as two halves that share the same contracts: 1. Bingocle on chain — the trust + innovation layer. Seven Solidity contracts on Mantle, the AI Speech Oracle, and a public Next.js web app. 2. Bingocle Capability — the consumer surface. A Minds Bazaar skill that lets anyone create or join an event, submit words, buy positions,

title: Quick Start
headings: Step 1: Build, test, and deploy the contracts
text: Quick Start Get the full Bingocle stack — contracts, agent, capability, and web app — running in four steps. Prerequisites Node.js 18+ and npm Foundry ( forge , cast , anvil ): install guide A Gemini API key ( GEMINI API KEY ) for the Curator / Odds / Oracle agents A funded Mantle Sepolia private key for deploying and for the agent signer (chainId 5003 , RPC https://rpc.sepolia.mantle.xyz ) The contracts are already live on Mantle Sepolia (see Introduction). You only need to deploy your own copy if you want a fresh instance. Step 1: Build, test, and deploy the contracts Deploy to Mantle Sepolia. The script broadcasts Deploy.s.sol , then writes the resulting addresses into agent/.env , capability/.env , and frontend/.env.local for you: Deploy.s.sol wires every module into the EventFactory registry, registers AGENT ADDRESS as the ERC 8004 oracle identity, and designates it as the WordPool curator. Step 2: Run the agent service The agent hosts the three AI roles and signs on chain transactions with the oracle wallet. Step 3: Run the Capability (Telegram bot) Players talk to the bot; organizer / AI verbs ( /create , /finalize , /validate ) are forwarded to the agent service, while player verbs ( /buy , /card , /claim ) go directly on chain through per user demo wallets. Step 4: Run the web app scripts/deploy.sh already populated frontend/.env.local with the NEXT PUBLIC contract add

title: The Game Loop
text: The Game Loop This guide walks the full Bingocle event lifecycle end to end, naming the exact contract function and the surface (agent HTTP route, capability command, or frontend action) that triggers each step. Every phase is derived purely from on chain timestamps by EventFactory.phaseOf , so the schedule is contract enforced rather than advanced by any privileged caller. Phases are strictly ordered and each has positive width. createEvent requires block.timestamp = disputeEnd settle, redeem, claim, sweep WordMarket, RewardVault Curation must commit the pool before marketLock ( commitPool reverts with MarketOpen otherwise), and RewardVault.fund is only allowed in Submission , Founder , or Market . The Thirteen Steps 1. Event creation. The organizer — in practice the agent wallet, via the capability create command which posts POST /events — calls EventFactory.createEvent(token, submissionEnd, founderEnd, marketLock, eventEnd, disputeEnd, maxWordsPerUser, cardSize, founderSeedUnit, bonus) . cardSize must be 25 (else BadCardSize ); a zero maxWordsPerUser defaults to 3 . See EventFactory. 2. Auto fund. Immediately after create, the agent server calls RewardVault.fund(eventId, amount) to seed the bonus and founder seed pool. The amount defaults to 30 MNT via body.rewardFundMnt or the REWARD FUND MNT env var ( 0 skips it). Funding must happen before the event goes Live. See RewardV

title: Local Development
text: Local Development A practical guide to building, testing, and exercising Bingocle on your own machine — from compiling the contracts with Foundry to driving the full game loop against a local chain, then seeding a live testnet demo with bot generated activity. Build And Test The Contracts The contracts/ package uses Foundry (configured in contracts/foundry.toml : solc 0.8.28, via ir = true , optimizer runs = 200 , evm version = "cancun" ). forge test runs 22 tests across test/Bingocle.t.sol and test/Market.t.sol . The market suite includes a fuzz test ( testFuzz ReserveSolvent ) and a stateful solvency invariant ( invariant balanceEqualsReserve ) that asserts the WordMarket contract balance always equals its tracked reserve across randomized call sequences — the core guarantee that trading payouts can never overdraw the pool. The invariant is what makes the bonding curve market safe to trust: no sequence of buys, sells, and redeems can leave the contract insolvent. See /guides/trust model. End To End Local Harness agent/scripts/e2e local.ts (npm script e2e:local ) drives the entire lifecycle against a local anvil node. It exercises the real agent ethers wiring — createEvent struct/tuple encoding, EventCreated log parsing, and every market call — against deployed bytecode, with no Gemini and no testnet . Because anvil lets you control block time, the harness uses evm setNextBloc

title: Security Notes
text: Security Notes An honest accounting of Bingocle's security posture. This is a hackathon build deployed to the Mantle Sepolia testnet ; the contracts and services have not been externally audited, and several deliberate shortcuts trade safety for demo velocity. Read this alongside the Trust Model before assuming anything about production readiness. Bingocle is testnet only. Never point a wallet, mnemonic, or key holding real value at any part of this stack. Threat Summary The most consequential risks come from the operator controlled off chain services (the agent HTTP API and the custodial demo wallets), not the on chain market math. The contracts are conservative — ReentrancyGuard everywhere, checks effects interactions, fee on transfer guards, and a path independent reserve invariant — but they have no pause switch and no on chain access control on the AI roles beyond a single key. Severity Area Issue Mitigation / Status HIGH Custodial wallets walletFor() in capability/src/wallets.ts derives every user wallet from one DEMO WALLET MNEMONIC via non hardened deriveChild(idx) . A single mnemonic leak compromises all user funds. Testnet only; enforced by operator discipline, not code — there is no mainnet/chainId guard. HIGH Agent API POST /events has no auth, open CORS ( CORS ORIGIN defaults to ), and auto funds the reward pool from the hot wallet on every call. It can be looped t

title: Trust Model
text: Trust Model Bingocle runs AI inference off chain but settles money on chain. This page traces how an off chain verdict becomes canonical truth, names exactly where trust sits, and is clear eyed about what is not verified versus what is provably sound. The Verdict to Truth Pipeline A Gemini inference produces a verdict off chain, but inference alone changes nothing. Only an on chain write makes a verdict canonical. The service agent/src/services/oracleRun.ts ( validateTranscript ) does the gating: It reads the current validatedBitmap(eventId) so it never re commits an already validated word. It runs matchTranscript over the pool, then filters to confidence = minConfidence ( minConf = args.minConfidence ?? 0.6 — default 0.6 ). For each fresh, confident match it scales confidence to the 1e4 fixed point ( Math.round(m.confidence SCALE) ) and calls OracleRegistry.commitValidation(eventId, wordIndex, confidence, snippet) , awaiting the receipt. OracleRegistry is the single source of on chain truth . commitValidation (in contracts/src/OracleRegistry.sol ) sets the verdict, then flips one bit: Everything downstream reads only that bitmap, never the AI: Reader What it reads File WordMarket.settle / redeem oracle().validatedBitmap(eventId) contracts/src/WordMarket.sol BingoCardNFT.markedMask IOracleRegistry(...).validatedBitmap(eventId) contracts/src/BingoCardNFT.sol So the chain trusts
